rationale: All five deviations are legitimate spec-mandated consequences, not masked regressions. A: plan.md fixture is purely mechanical (Leg 3 requires a readable plan); assertions in all ~13 tests are byte-identical otherwise. B: open->closed is a forced, correct consequence of A activating the pre-existing (unchanged) gate-1/3 bead-status loop, which A silently bypassed before (planErr!=nil skipped it). C (the key one): traced readPlanBeadIDs — it never returns nil-err with empty bead_ids, so the old CommitCount-preflight 'no commits beyond main' message was only ever reachable when planErr!=nil. Pre-Bead-2 that meant a missing plan.md WITH real commits sailed through unblocked (enforcement gate 1/3 silently skips, CommitCount check only fires at count==0) - exactly the hole Bead 2 closes. Post-fix, Leg 3 refuses unconditionally and earlier (pre-mutation), which is strictly safer, not a regression. Verified TestApproveImpl_NoCommitsButClosedBeads_AllowsCleanup (valid plan + 0 commits) still passes unblocked, confirming nothing real was lost. D: confirmed in divergence.go:113-121 that CheckADRDivergence silently no-ops on missing plan.md - OverrideADR neutralizes a newly-activated unrelated gate; doc-sync WARN + FinalizeEpic assertions unchanged. E: implBranchExistsFn matches the implXxxFn seam convention and Leg 3's branch-truthful recovery wording. All 6 target packages pass (go test -count=1); named tests pass individually; git status clean except the review-output dir.

findings.A_plan_fixture_addition: ADDRESSED/legitimate — mechanical accommodation for Leg 3's fail-closed plan read; assertions in all ~13 tests unchanged.
findings.B_open_to_closed_default: ADDRESSED/legitimate — necessary consequence of A: with a plan.md now present, the pre-existing (unchanged-by-this-bead) bead-status loop at impl.go:271-287 actually runs and would refuse on 'open', so the fixture must model a ready-for-impl-approve spec.
findings.C_NoCommitsNoBeads_assertion_change: ADDRESSED/legitimate, KEY deviation confirmed correct. readPlanBeadIDs (impl.go:510-531) never returns (beadIDs=[], err=nil) — empty bead_ids always yields an error. Therefore the old CommitCount-preflight condition `count==0 && (planErr!=nil || len(beadIDs)==0)` could only ever fire when planErr!=nil, i.e. only on a missing/corrupt plan.md. Pre-Bead-2, a missing plan.md with real commits present was NOT caught by anything (gate 1/3 silently skips on planErr!=nil; CommitCount check only fires at count==0) — an un-gated hole. Bead 2's Leg 3 now refuses unconditionally on planErr!=nil, earlier and before any mutation (epic close/phase write), which is strictly safer. TestApproveImpl_NoCommitsButClosedBeads_AllowsCleanup (valid plan, CommitCountResult:0) still passes with no error, proving the legitimate 0-commits-but-valid-plan 'cleanup' path is untouched. Minor non-blocking nit: the CommitCount preflight's `planErr != nil` disjunct (impl.go:424) is now dead code (always intercepted earlier by Leg 3) — harmless, pre-existing line left unmodified by this bead, worth a follow-up cleanup but not a correctness or regression issue.
findings.D_OverrideADR_addition: ADDRESSED/legitimate — confirmed in internal/validate/divergence.go:113-121 that CheckADRDivergence silently no-ops when plan.md is absent (documented, pre-existing behavior identical to its spec.md-absent handling). Adding a plan.md for Leg 3 incidentally activates this previously-dormant gate for the test's uncovered cmd/ fixture; OverrideADR is scoped only to that unrelated gate. The doc-sync WARN rendering and the FinalizeEpic-called assertions the test actually pins are byte-identical to before.
findings.E_seventh_seam: ADDRESSED/legitimate — implBranchExistsFn = gitutil.BranchExists follows the same implXxxFn seam-naming convention as the other six, is wired only into implObligationRefusal's branch-state-truthful recovery (Leg 3), and is functionally distinct from the existing fail-open bool probe inside implScanOrphansFn (R1) per the plan's C+B-unchanged requirement.
